title: Finite-to-unbounded family audit
purpose: Represent the activation-critical question of whether finite certificate schemas uniformly cover all SAT input sizes in polynomial time, without treating bounded seed evidence as unrestricted final soundness.
scopeQuestion: Does every finite certificate schema uniformly cover all SAT input sizes in polynomial time?

requiredUniformityCriteria.description: The schema must quantify over an unbounded SAT/NAND input family rather than a fixed finite fixture universe.
There must be an explicit generator that maps every input size and input instance to certificate data.
The generator, certificate size, checker replay, and downstream construction must have polynomial bounds in the input size.
The generated certificate family must preserve the NAND semantics, SAT threshold condition, and mode-firewall discipline for every input size.
No finite bounded-model pass may be silently generalized to all input sizes without a checked uniform schema.

currentVerdict.reason: The audit makes the finite-to-unbounded question explicit and ties it to the active release blocker, but it does not yet provide the required uniform polynomial certificate-family proof.

nonClaims: This audit does not activate public theorem emission.
This audit does not clear Release.UnrestrictedFinalSoundness or ExternalReview.Acceptance.
This audit does not prove that bounded small-model evidence covers all SAT input sizes.
This audit records the uniformity criteria that a later unrestricted final-soundness checker must satisfy.
